summary: Install-time credential and environment exfiltration in @emcd-vue/b2b-pay-form
details: The package declares postinstall: node scripts/postinstall.js, and scripts/postinstall.js is a 166 KB obfuscator.io-style bundle (string-array + numeric-byte-array decoders) that runs automatically on npm install. Decoded behavior harvests installer host identity (USERDOMAIN, COMPUTERNAME, PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE, PROGRAMDATA, APPDATA, LOCALAPPDATA, TEMP, npm_config_user_agent, NODE_OPTIONS), OS/network info from the os module, and iterates process.env matching each key against a large decoded table of credential-name patterns to locate secrets in the installer's environment. Additional filesystem probes read decoded paths under the user's home / APPDATA / LOCALAPPDATA (consistent with shell rc/history, npm/aws/ssh config, wallet/keystore files). The collected data is XOR/RC4-style encoded and POSTed via a runtime-decoded https/http require to an author-controlled endpoint whose hostname is also assembled from decoded byte arrays. Node built-in modules (os, fs, path, crypto, https/http, child_process) are loaded via require(decode([...])) to hide the module graph from static readers. Package metadata mimics dotenv (description: 'Loads environment variables from.env file') and uses the internal-looking scope @emcd-vue/* with a fabricated emcd-vue.io homepage and a README instructing installers to point.npmrc at https://npm.emcd-vue.io — the canonical dependency-confusion shape targeting any organization whose real internal scope collides with @emcd-vue.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
